name: OmopObservationMeasurementView
title: Observation → measurement (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
Labs and vitals with numeric/coded results.

Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce a flat row, one per FHIR Observation, preserving raw FHIR values. Code→concept_id resolution happens in stage 2 by joining the (code_system, code_value) pair against vocab.concept.

Kept compact: one column per concept-position (not one per vocabulary). The runtime resolver in stage 2 uses mapspec/profiles/system-aliases.json to map FHIR system URLs to OMOP vocabulary_id.

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Observation.id; → surrogate measurement_id (hash/sequence in stage 2).

column.name: subject_id
column.description: FHIR reference string ('Patient/<id>' or 'urn:uuid:<id>'). Resolve to person_id by trailing segment.

column.name: code_system
column.description: Code system URL of the first coding (typically http://loinc.org). Mapped to OMOP vocabulary_id via system-aliases.

column.name: code_value
column.description: Source code → measurement_concept_id (after Maps-to). Also kept verbatim as measurement_source_value.

column.name: code_text
column.description: Free-text label → fallback measurement_source_value when code_value is missing.

column.name: effective_dt
column.description: effectiveDateTime branch of effective[x]. → measurement_date / measurement_datetime.

column.name: effective_period_start
column.description: effectivePeriod.start branch. Used if effective_dt is absent.

column.name: value_comparator
column.description: Quantity comparator (<, <=, >=, >). → operator_concept_id via tiny lookup.

column.name: value_unit_code
column.description: UCUM unit code. → unit_concept_id (joined as system='http://unitsofmeasure.org').

column.name: value_code_system
column.description: When value[x] is a CodeableConcept: system URL of first coding. → maps with code_value to value_as_concept_id.

column.name: range_low
column.description: First reference range lower bound. → range_low.

column.name: range_high
column.description: First reference range upper bound. → range_high.
